picks.rationale: The Tampa Bay Rays are in excellent form, riding a four-game winning streak and looking to complete a home sweep against the Diamondbacks. Rays starting pitcher Drew Rasmussen boasts a strong 2.62 ERA and 0.88 WHIP, while Arizona's Merrill Kelly is struggling with a 5.71 ERA. Historically, the Rays have dominated this matchup, winning 9 of their last 10 against the Diamondbacks and covering the run line in 8 of their last 9 head-to-head contests. The Diamondbacks have also lost their last seven road games against American League opponents.

The Miami Marlins are playing exceptional baseball in June, holding an 18-5 record this month and having already secured their first season-series win over the Cardinals since 2016. Miami's offense is locked in, led by Otto Lopez's .336 batting average, and their pitching staff has stifled St. Louis in the first two games of the series. The Marlins have a strong head-to-head record against the Cardinals, winning 5 of their last 6 meetings and their last four visits to St. Louis. With Tyler Phillips (3.09 ERA) on the mound for Miami against Kyle Leahy (4.24 ERA) for St. Louis, the Marlins have a pitching edge.

The Philadelphia Phillies face a Mets team that will be utilizing a bullpen game after heavily taxing their relievers in the previous day's contest. Phillies starter Jesus Luzardo has historically dominated the Mets, holding a 5-1 record with a 2.37 ERA in his last eight starts against them, and his underlying metrics suggest positive regression is likely. The Mets' offense has been one of the worst in the league this season, ranking near the bottom in runs, hits, batting average, slugging, and OPS. Furthermore, 11 of the Phillies' last 14 wins have been by two or more runs, making the run line an attractive option.

This rubber match features two strong starting pitchers in Emerson Hancock (3.60 ERA, 1.02 WHIP) and Gavin Williams (3.82 ERA, 9-4 record). Both offenses have been struggling significantly, with the Mariners scoring three runs or fewer in four of their last five games and both teams batting below .231. The Guardians are also without star third baseman José Ramírez due to injury, further hindering their offensive production. The total has seen significant line movement, dropping from an opening of 7.5 runs to 6.5, indicating sharp money on the Under.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ San Diego Padres
picks.rationale: Despite the Padres' overall offensive struggles, the pitching matchup points towards a higher-scoring affair. Dodgers starter Emmet Sheehan has been inconsistent with a 5.32 ERA and a 1.30 WHIP, and the Dodgers have lost his last four starts. While Padres starter Michael King has a solid ERA, his advanced metrics suggest some shakiness. The Dodgers boast the league's best offense, capable of putting up runs against any pitcher, and they just scored 15 runs in the previous game. Both teams combined for 8 and 18 runs in the previous two games, and the Over has hit in the Dodgers' last five road games against NL opponents.
